name: Ka'ena Point Trail
tagline: Oahu's raw western tip — monk seals laze on rocks and albatross nest where the island ends.
parking: Free lot at end of Farrington Hwy past Yokohama Bay
hours: Sunrise to sunset daily (no gates on the Waianae side)
bestTime: Early morning to beat the heat; Nov–Jun for albatross nesting season
insiderTips: Bring at least 2 liters of water per person — there is absolutely zero shade for the entire 5-mile round trip. Wear a hat and sun protection. The fenced sanctuary at the point protects nesting Laysan albatross and Hawaiian monk seals — stay behind the fence. Dolphins sometimes visible from the point. Do NOT attempt to swim here — the currents are extremely dangerous.
